title: Thermia fabacea
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Thermia fabacea". However, I found a plant called "Thermopsis fabacea" also known as "Yarrowleaf Thermopsis", which is a perennial herb native to North America. 

Here's a paragraph about Thermopsis fabacea:

Thermopsis fabacea, also known as Yarrowleaf Thermopsis, is a perennial herb native to North America, belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Thermopsis fabacea include isoflavones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Thermopsis fabacea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
